San Bruno Mountain State & County Park. Ridge Trail in San Bruno Mountain Park. Summit Loop Trail in San Bruno Mountain Park. San Bruno Mountain Park is a landmark of local and regional significance, standing as a unique open-space island in the midst of the peninsula's urbanization at the northern end of the Santa Cruz Mountain Range. The Mountain's ridge line runs in an east-west configuration, with considerable slopes and elevations ranging from 250 feet to 1,314 feet at the summit. The 2,416 acres of rugged landscape offer excellent hiking opportunities and outstanding views of San Francisco and Central Bay Area. San Bruno Mountain Park Ecosystem Health & Fuel Reduction. Work is underway in San Bruno Mountain Park to increase ecosystem health and reduce potential for dangerous wildfires. title: San Bruno Mountain State Park
content: # San Bruno Mountain State Park. At the northern reaches of the Santa Cruz Mountains range, this park is home to a wide variety of birds and animals and boasts excellent hiking opportunities and outstanding views of San Francisco and the Bay Area. #### California State Parks Day Use and Entry Fees for San Bruno Mountain State Park. Turn west on Guadalupe Canyon Parkway toward the Mountain and go to the park entrance. At Guadalupe Canyon Parkway, turn right and go eastbound to the park entrance. **Upcoming Events** All Events for San Bruno Mountain State Park. The park provides Bay Area visitors with day-use facilities, hiking trails, and beautiful views of the city and the bay. Radio Road, leading up from near the main entrance, affords visitors the opportunity to drive to the summit of the Mountain and enjoy breathtaking views north to Mt. Tamalpais, south to Mt. Diablo, east to the Oakland foothills, and west to the Farallon Islands.

title: SAN BRUNO MOUNTAIN STATE & COUNTY PARK - Yelp
content: Beautiful county/state park located just south of the city. The park has plenty of trails, a nice big grassy area, and some picnic tables.
